assumptions: Assume the declared dividend has created a recognised intragroup receivable/payable and is denominated in a foreign currency for at least one group entity.
Assume the analysis is limited to hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9, with IFRIC 16 considered only for net investment hedges.
Assume the key eligibility question is whether foreign exchange gains or losses on the intragroup item are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Hedge of intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup dividend receivable/payable is analysed as an intragroup monetary item, which can qualify in consolidated financial statements only for foreign currency risk that is not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Hedge of forecast intragroup transaction
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately permits certain highly probable forecast intragroup transactions in consolidation when the foreign currency risk affects consolidated profit or loss, which is the main pre-recognition alternative to a receivable-based analysis.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 sets out a distinct consolidated hedge model for foreign currency risk on a net investment in a foreign operation, which defines an important alternative boundary to hedging an intragroup dividend receivable itself.

approaches.label: No qualifying hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The default IFRS 9 position in consolidated financial statements is that intragroup items do not qualify as hedged items unless a specific intragroup foreign currency exception applies.
